sovereign_credit_rating.value: S&P: B-, Moody's: Caa1, Fitch: B-

resource_self_sufficiency_index.methodology: Average of min(production/consumption, 1.0) for energy, food, water, minerals

political_risk_premium_bps.methodology: Sovereign rating implied spread + adjustments for instability, conflict, sanctions, autocracy

market_accessibility_score.methodology: Average of capital_account_openness, stock_market_cap/200, financial_dev_index, ease_of_business/100

narrative.executive_summary: Nigeria faces a worsening security crisis after Boko Haram massacred 170+ civilians in Kwara state on Feb 3 -- one of the deadliest attacks in years. The US deployed 200 troops for counterterrorism cooperation. Inflation at 23% with CCC+/Caa1 credit rating and 1,250 bps risk premium. Nigeria Labour Congress protests continue over insecurity and economic conditions. Oil price surge benefits government revenues but insecurity threatens production in the Niger Delta.
key_changes_this_week: Boko Haram massacred 170+ civilians in Kwara state -- one of deadliest attacks in years (major)
US deployed 200 troops to Nigeria for counterterrorism cooperation
Nigeria Labour Congress protests over insecurity and economic conditions continue
Oil price +13% surge benefits government revenues but insecurity threatens production
narrative.outlook: Nigeria's security situation is deteriorating with Boko Haram expanding geographic reach into Kwara state (previously considered relatively safe). The Tinubu government faces simultaneous security and economic reform challenges. Oil revenue benefits from price surge but production faces insecurity risk in the Niger Delta. EndBadGovernance movement and labor unrest add social instability pressure ahead of 2027 elections.
narrative.investor_implications: Nigeria sovereign bonds face continued pressure from security deterioration and high inflation. Naira remains volatile. Oil equities (Seplat, Oando) benefit from price surge but face production risk. Bank stocks face NPL pressure from economic headwinds. US troop deployment signals deepening security partnership but also highlights governance gaps.
narrative.data_quality_note: Nigeria macro data is of medium quality. Security incident reporting has geographic and temporal gaps. Oil production data is from NNPC and may lag.
